content.full_text:
LAW OFFICES OF

RICHARD L. MOOMAU
TIMOTHY J. STAPFEL
ALLEN R. BALL

MOOMAU & STAPFEL
1001 SOUTH BROADWAY
SANTA MARIA, CALIFORNIA 93454-6605
(805) 928-0811

231 N. DOS CAMINOS AVE
VENTURA, CALIFORNIA 93003
(805) 656-0356
(805) 653-1855

IN REPLY REFER
TO OUR FILE NO

October 9, 1990

Santa Maria

Louisa Mascheca, Esq.
Legal Division
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. Box 807
428 "J" Street, Suite 800
Sacramento, CA 95814

RE: Confirmation of Prior FPPC Advice re Full Disclosure

Dear Ms. Mascheca:

     This correspondence is intended to confirm the substance of
our telephone conversation of October 9, 1990 regarding the above-
referenced matter as it relates to a campaign for County Supervisor
in Santa Barbara County.

     As I indicated during our telephone conversation, I am
representing an individual who is a candidate for County Supervisor
in Santa Barbara County. As part of the campaign effort we have
sought to fully comply with all applicable campaign law and
regulations. In this spirit, prior to completing our initial
campaign financial statement (Form 490), we contacted the FPPC
Technical Division to inquire as to the proper method of reporting
and identifying campaign contributors consistent with Government
Code, Section 84211(j) and indicated that we wanted to provide the
fullest disclosure possible. At that time we were informed that
the listing of a local address for all campaign contributors was
clearly consistent with the law and, specifically, met the
requirements of the code section cited above.

     During our telephone conversation of October 9, 1990 you
reiterated the position of the FPPC that such listing was proper,
correct and clearly consistent with the statutory scheme regulating
campaign reporting in California.

     As I explained to you during our telephone conversation, the
campaign's intent in listing a local address for contributors was
for the purpose of supplying the most complete campaign disclosure
possible so that any member of the public interested in reviewing
the campaign reports would immediately be aware of a contributor's
interests and/or assets within the relevant jurisdiction in which
the election is being conducted. You replied that such a full
disclosure policy on the part of this campaign is not only fully
consistent with applicable California law but above and beyond the
legal reporting requirements and, therefore, additional complete
disclosure on our part.

Louisa Mascheca
Fair Political Practices Commission
October 9, 1990
Page Two


This campaign has adopted this complete disclosure policy from
the outset of the campaign and because candidate, Mike Stoker, is fully
committed to this State's campaign reporting laws and the public's
right to know. In light of this, Mr. Stoker has maintained a strong
policy in providing financial information in a way which is clearly
consistent with not only the letter of the law but with the spirit
in which it was enacted.

Therefore, local addresses of contributors are provided to
ensure that the public is fully advised of any contributor's
interests and/or assets in the local area. If such a full
voluntary disclosure practice were not followed, present law allows
a candidate to merely list a contributor's principal place of
business, which may have no readily identifiable connection to the
area where the election is being conducted, without disclosing the
contributor's interest within the political jurisdiction most
relevant to the election.

In response to a question you raised about the possibility of
one or more of our contributors being upset with this campaign's
voluntary full disclosure policy, I indicated to you that we
informed contributors at the outset that we intended to comply with
this voluntary full disclosure policy and we would not accept
contributions from those who did not agree with us that the
public's right to know is paramount.

While it is not often that your office receives correspondence
from a campaign which insists upon going beyond what the reporting
laws require to promote a commitment to the public's right to know,
this campaign believes in leadership by example and has done so
throughout this campaign effort and not only in the area of full
disclosure of campaign financing and contributions.

Please feel free to contact me with any questions you may have
regarding this matter.

Very truly yours,

TIMOTHY J. STAFFEL
